member.name: T:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit
member.summary: Represents a composite control that combines a text-editable drop-down list with a customizable appearance, providing functionality similar to a with additional styling and event support.
member.remarks: exposes events and properties for customizing the look and behavior of the drop-down button, border, and list items. It supports data binding, auto-complete features, and right-to-left layout. The control is designed for use in Windows Forms applications where a styled, editable drop-down selection is required. Most -related operations and properties are accessible through this control. Thread safety is not guaranteed; access the control only from the UI thread.

member.name: E:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.DropDown
member.summary: Occurs when the drop-down portion of a is shown.

member.name: E:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.SelectedIndexChanged
member.summary: Occurs when the property has changed.

member.name: E:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.SelectionChangeCommitted
member.summary: Occurs when the user changes the selected item and that change is displayed in the .

member.name: E:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.TextUpdate
member.summary: Occurs when the control has formatted the text, but before the text is displayed.

member.name: E:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.DropDownClosed
member.summary: Occurs when the drop-down portion of the is no longer visible.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.DrawSeparator
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether a separator line is drawn alongside the button.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.BorderColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the color of the border displayed around the control.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.DropDownMouseOverBackColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the background color of the drop-down button when the mouse pointer is over it.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.DropDownMouseDownBackColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the background color of the drop-down button when the mouse button is pressed.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.BackColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the background color of the control.
member.remarks: Setting this property changes the background color of both the control and its inner elements. If not set, the default value is the system window color.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.ForeColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreground color of the control.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.DropDownArrowColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the color used to draw the drop-down arrow on the button.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.RightToLeft
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether text and child elements are displayed from right to left, such as for languages like Arabic or Hebrew.
member.remarks: Setting this property affects the layout and alignment of the control and its child elements to support right-to-left languages. Changing the value may also update the position of associated UI elements to match the reading direction.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.Items
member.summary: Gets the collection of items contained in the ComboBox.
member.remarks: Use this property to add, remove, or access the items displayed in the ComboBox. The collection supports standard collection operations such as adding, removing, and enumerating items. Modifying the collection updates the items shown in the ComboBox control.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.DataSource
member.summary: Gets or sets the data source that populates the items in the combo box.
member.remarks: The data source can be any object that implements the , , or interfaces, such as a , , or an array. Setting this property enables data binding for the combo box. When the data source is set, the items collection is managed by the data source and cannot be modified directly. To display specific properties from complex objects, set the and properties accordingly.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.AutoCompleteMode
member.summary: Gets or sets the automatic completion behavior of the text box as the user types.
member.remarks: Use this property to control how suggestions are provided to the user based on their input. The available modes determine whether suggestions are shown in a drop-down list, appended to the existing text, or both. The default value is .

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.AutoCompleteSource
member.summary: Gets or sets the source from which the control retrieves auto-complete suggestions for the text entered by the user.
member.remarks: Use this property to specify whether the control should suggest and/or append possible matches based on a predefined list, file system entries, or other sources. The behavior of auto-complete is also influenced by the and properties.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.AutoCompleteCustomSource
member.summary: Gets or sets the custom string collection used to provide auto-complete suggestions for the text box.
member.remarks: Use this property to specify a custom list of strings that will be suggested to the user as they type, when the auto-complete feature is enabled. The collection can be modified at runtime to update the available suggestions. This property is typically used in conjunction with the and properties.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.DisplayMember
member.summary: Gets or sets the property to display for this .
member.returns: A specifying the name of an object property that is contained in the collection specified by the property. The default is an empty string ("").

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.ValueMember
member.summary: Gets or sets the path of the property to use as the actual value for the items in the .
member.returns: A representing a single property name of the property value, or a hierarchy of period-delimited property names that resolves to a property name of the final data-bound object. The default is an empty string ("").
member.exception: The specified property path cannot be resolved through the object specified by the property.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.Sorted
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the items in the combo box are sorted alphabetically.
member.remarks: When set to , items added to the combo box are automatically sorted in ascending order. Setting this property to preserves the order in which items are added.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.SelectedIndex
member.summary: Gets or sets the zero-based index of the currently selected item in the combo box.
member.remarks: Set this property to -1 to clear the selection. If the value is less than -1 or greater than or equal to the number of items in the combo box, an exception may be thrown.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.SelectedItem
member.summary: Gets or sets the currently selected item in the control.
member.remarks: Setting this property to a value that does not exist in the item collection will clear the selection. The value can be null if no item is selected.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.MaxDropDownItems
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of items to display in the drop-down portion of the combo box.
member.remarks: If the number of items in the combo box exceeds this value, a vertical scroll bar is displayed in the drop-down list. The default value is 8.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.MaxLength
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of characters the user can enter into the text box component of the combo box.
member.remarks: A value of 0 indicates that there is no limit to the number of characters that can be entered. Setting this property to a value less than 0 will throw an exception.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.SelectionLength
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of characters selected in the text of the combo box.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.SelectionStart
member.summary: Gets or sets the starting position of the text selection within the editable portion of the combo box.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.Text
member.summary: Gets or sets the text associated with this control.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.FormattingEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether formatting is applied to the property of the .
member.returns: if formatting of the property is enabled; otherwise, . The default is .

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.DropDownWidth
member.summary: Gets or sets the width of the of the drop-down portion of a combo box.
member.returns: The width, in pixels, of the drop-down box.
member.exception: The specified value is less than one.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.RenderFontFamilyItems
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether font family items are custom rendered in the combo box.
member.remarks: Set to true to enable owner-drawn rendering of font family items, allowing for customized appearance.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.FindString(System.String)
member.summary: Finds the first item in the list that starts with the specified string and returns its index.
member.remarks: The search starts at the beginning of the list and wraps around to the beginning if necessary. The method performs a case-insensitive comparison using the current culture.
member.param: The string to search for. The comparison is case-insensitive. Can be or empty to search for an empty string.
member.returns: The zero-based index of the first matching item; otherwise, -1 if no match is found.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.FindString(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Finds the first item in the list that starts with the specified string, beginning the search at the given index.
member.param: The string to search for. The search is case-insensitive and matches items that start with this string.
The zero-based index at which to start the search. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than the number of items in the list.
member.returns: The zero-based index of the first item found that starts with the specified string; otherwise, -1 if no matching item is found.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.FindStringExact(System.String)
member.summary: Finds the first item in the combo box that exactly matches the specified string.
member.param: The string to search for. The comparison is case-insensitive and must match the entire item text.
member.returns: The zero-based index of the first item that exactly matches the specified string; otherwise, -1 if no match is found.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.FindStringExact(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Finds the first item in the list that exactly matches the specified string, starting the search at the given index.
member.param: The string to search for. The comparison is case-insensitive and must match the entire item text.
The zero-based index at which to start the search. Must be greater than or equal to -1 and less than the number of items.
member.returns: The zero-based index of the first item that exactly matches the specified string; otherwise, -1 if no match is found.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.Focus
member.summary: Sets input focus to the control, if it is focusable.
member.remarks: If the control is not enabled or visible, calling this method has no effect. This method overrides the base implementation to direct focus to the underlying ComboBox control.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.GetItemHeight(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves the height, in pixels, of the item at the specified index within the combo box.
member.param: The zero-based index of the item whose height is to be retrieved. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than the total number of items.
member.returns: The height, in pixels, of the specified item.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.GetItemText(System.Object)
member.summary: Returns the display text for the specified item as it would appear in the combo box.
member.param: The item for which to retrieve the display text. Can be null if the combo box supports null items.
member.returns: A string representing the display text of the specified item. Returns an empty string if the item is null or has no display text.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.Select
member.summary: Selects all text in the combo box, if any is present.
member.remarks: This method brings the text selection into focus, allowing users to quickly replace or copy the current text. If the combo box does not contain any text, calling this method has no effect.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.Select(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Selects a range of text within the editable portion of the combo box.
member.remarks: If the combo box is not editable, this method has no effect. The selection will be adjusted if the specified range exceeds the available text length.
member.param: The zero-based index of the first character in the selection.
The number of characters to select.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.SelectAll
member.summary: Selects all the text in the editable portion of the combo box, if any.
member.remarks: If the combo box is not editable or contains no text, this method has no effect.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.BeginUpdate
member.summary: Maintains performance when items are added to the one at a time.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownEdit.EndUpdate
member.summary: Resumes painting the control after painting is suspended by the method.

member.name: T:Salem.Controls.SalDropDownList
member.summary: Represents a custom dropdown list control that combines a button and a combo box, providing enhanced appearance customization and selection functionality.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFontDropDownEdit.ForeColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreground color of the control.
member.remarks: Setting this property changes the color of the text displayed by the control. If the control contains an inner ComboBox, its foreground color is also updated to match.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFontDropDownEdit.RightToLeft
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether text and child elements are displayed from right to left, such as for languages like Arabic or Hebrew.
member.remarks: Setting this property affects the layout and alignment of the control and its child elements to support right-to-left languages. Changing the value may also update the position of associated UI elements to match the reading direction.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalFontDropDownEdit.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the SalFontDropDownEdit class.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFontDropDownList.ForeColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreground color of the control.
member.remarks: Setting this property changes the color of the text displayed by the control. If the control contains an inner ComboBox, its foreground color is also updated to match.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFontDropDownList.RightToLeft
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether text and child elements are displayed from right to left, such as for languages like Arabic or Hebrew.
member.remarks: Setting this property affects the layout and alignment of the control and its child elements to support right-to-left languages. Changing the value may also update the position of associated UI elements to match the reading direction.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalFontDropDownList.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the SalFontDropDownEdit class.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalSplitButton.BackColor
member.summary: This property is meaningless for this control

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalSplitButton.Padding
member.summary: This property is meaningless for this control.

member.name: T:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox
member.summary: Represents a custom control box for a form, providing minimize, maximize/restore, and close window actions with customizable button icons and enablement.
member.remarks: Use this control to add standard window control buttons to a custom form, especially when the default form border is hidden or replaced. The control allows customization of the button icons via Unicode characters and enables or disables each action independently. It is typically anchored to the top-right corner of the parent form. The control automatically interacts with its parent form to perform window state changes and closing actions.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.CloseChar
member.summary: Gets or sets the character displayed on the close button.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.MaximizeChar
member.summary: Gets or sets the character displayed on the maximize button when the parent form is in the normal window state.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.MinimizeChar
member.summary: Gets or sets the character displayed on the minimize button.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.RestoreChar
member.summary: Gets or sets the character displayed on the maximize button when the parent form is maximized.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.EnableMinimize
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the minimize button is enabled and visible.
member.remarks: Setting this property to ensures that the minimize button is both enabled and visible. Setting it to disables and hides the minimize button. Changing this property may also affect the visibility of the maximize button.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.EnableMaximize
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the maximize button is enabled and visible on the window.
member.remarks: When set to , the maximize button is enabled and visible. When set to , the maximize button is disabled and its visibility depends on the value of . This property is typically used to control whether users can maximize the window.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.EnableClose
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the close button is enabled.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.CorrectLocation
member.summary: Adjusts the control's location so that it aligns with the top-right corner of its parent form's client area.
member.remarks: This method has no effect if the control does not have a parent form. Typically used to reposition the control after the parent form is resized or its layout changes.

member.name: F:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.components
member.summary: Required designer variable.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.Dispose(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Clean up any resources being used.
member.param: true if managed resources should be disposed; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalFormControlBox.InitializeComponent
member.summary: Required method for Designer support - do not modify the contents of this method with the code editor.

member.name: F:Salem.Controls.Internal_Utils.Frm_MessageBoxBase.components
member.summary: Required designer variable.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.Internal_Utils.Frm_MessageBoxBase.Dispose(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Clean up any resources being used.
member.param: true if managed resources should be disposed; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.Internal_Utils.Frm_MessageBoxBase.InitializeComponent
member.summary: Required method for Designer support - do not modify the contents of this method with the code editor.

member.name: T:Salem.Controls.SalOverlayPanel
member.summary: Provides static methods for displaying and removing a semi-transparent overlay panel on a Windows Form.
member.remarks: The overlay panel can be used to block user interaction with the underlying form, such as during loading operations or modal workflows. All members of this class are static and thread affinity with the UI thread must be maintained when calling these methods.

member.name: T:Salem.Controls.Properties.Resources
member.summary: A strongly-typed resource class, for looking up localized strings, etc.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.Properties.Resources.ResourceManager
member.summary: Returns the cached ResourceManager instance used by this class.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.Properties.Resources.Culture
member.summary: Overrides the current thread's CurrentUICulture property for all resource lookups using this strongly typed resource class.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.Properties.Resources.Item_Check
member.summary: Looks up a localized resource of type System.Drawing.Bitmap.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.Properties.Resources.MsgBx_Error
member.summary: Looks up a localized resource of type System.Drawing.Bitmap.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.Properties.Resources.MsgBx_Exclamation
member.summary: Looks up a localized resource of type System.Drawing.Bitmap.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.Properties.Resources.MsgBx_Question
member.summary: Looks up a localized resource of type System.Drawing.Bitmap.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.Properties.Resources.MsgBx_Warning
member.summary: Looks up a localized resource of type System.Drawing.Bitmap.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.Properties.Resources.ribbonGroupDialogLauncherLight
member.summary: Looks up a localized resource of type System.Drawing.Bitmap.

member.name: T:Salem.Controls.SalSelectableLabel
member.summary: Represents a label-like control that displays read-only, selectable text with optional parent color matching.
member.remarks: SalSelectableLabel is derived from TextBox and is designed to behave visually like a label while allowing optional text selection. By default, it disables editing, border, and tab stop, and matches its background and foreground colors to its parent control for seamless integration. The control can be configured to allow or prevent text selection and to inherit the parent's foreground color. This makes it suitable for scenarios where label-like appearance is desired but text selection may be needed, such as copying displayed information.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalSelectableLabel.TextSelectionEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether users can select text within the label using the mouse.
member.remarks: When text selection is enabled, the cursor changes to an I-beam and users can highlight and copy text. When disabled, the cursor appears as an arrow and text selection is not possible.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalSelectableLabel.ForeColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreground color of the control.
member.remarks: If the control is configured to match its parent's foreground color, setting this property will update the color based on the parent's value. Otherwise, the specified color is applied directly. Changes to this property may affect the appearance of text or graphics rendered by the control.

member.name: P:Salem.Controls.SalSelectableLabel.MatchParentForeColor
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the control's foreground color matches its parent control's foreground color.
member.remarks: When this property is set to , the control automatically updates its foreground color to match that of its parent. Setting this property to allows the control to use its own specified foreground color.

member.name: M:Salem.Controls.SalSelectableLabel.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the SalSelectableLabel class with default property values.
member.remarks: This constructor configures the control to be read-only, non-multiline, and disables tab stop and word wrapping by default. The background color is set to match the parent control if available. The caret is hidden when the control receives focus or when the mouse button is released over the control.
